Title: Set up Claude Code studio
Description: Epic: stand up the Claude Code 'studio' — agents, skills, hooks, MCP connectors, and the CLAUDE.md hierarchy — so the project is powerful and easy to drive. This epic operationalizes the architecture blueprint.

Title: Initialize Astro 6 + React 19.2 + Tailwind v4 project
Description: Scaffold the app: Astro 6 static-first, React 19.2 islands, Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first config). Confirm pnpm, strict TypeScript, clean dev server. Commit the baseline.

Title: Write and trim root CLAUDE.md
Description: Run /init, then trim CLAUDE.md to facts only: stack, commands, conventions, hard rules, agent/skill index. Keep under ~200 lines. Procedures go in skills, not here.

Title: Configure .claude/settings.json permissions
Description: Set allow/ask/deny: allow routine pnpm lint/test/build/tokens, ask on git push, deny reads of .env and secrets. Verify hooks are wired.

Title: Author the six studio subagents
Description: Create accessibility-reviewer (read-only, signature), design (generate+review), frontend-architect (Opus, plan-first), content-writer, devops-release, qa-test. Scope tools tightly; reviewers read-only.

Title: Author the workflow skills
Description: Create /scope-ticket, /adr, /sync-tokens, /seed-linear, /seed-notion. Seed skills are manual-invoke only.

Title: Add PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks
Description: guard-bash.sh blocks destructive rm and secret reads; format-and-lint.sh runs Prettier + ESLint (jsx-a11y) after edits. Confirm both fire.

Title: Set up the design token pipeline
Description: Epic: DTCG tokens → Style Dictionary v4 → CSS variables + Tailwind v4 theme, with the three-tier (primitive/semantic/component) architecture.

Title: Configure Tokens Studio to DTCG and sync to repo
Description: Set Tokens Studio output to W3C DTCG. Sync tokens/*.json into the repo as the source of truth.
Parent: Set up the design token pipeline

Title: Wire Style Dictionary v4 build
Description: Add Style Dictionary v4 + @tokens-studio/sd-transforms. Output src/styles/tokens.css and the Tailwind theme. Expose as pnpm tokens.
Parent: Set up the design token pipeline

Title: Document token naming and pipeline
Description: Write tokens/CLAUDE.md and an ADR on the token architecture. Set Figma variable code syntax to match token names.
Parent: Set up the design token pipeline

Title: Build the component system on React Aria
Description: Epic: leaf primitives on React Aria Components, consuming tokens, documented in Storybook 10.

Title: Build leaf primitives
Description: Button, Input, Text, Badge, Icon on React Aria Components + tokens. These cascade through Code Connect coverage, so do them first.
Parent: Build the component system on React Aria

Title: Stand up Storybook 10 with a11y + vitest addons
Description: Configure Storybook 10 with addon-a11y (axe) and addon-vitest. Add src/components/CLAUDE.md.
Parent: Build the component system on React Aria

Title: Set up Figma Code Connect for primitives
Description: Map Figma components to the leaf primitives via Code Connect (seat permitting). Improves design-to-code accuracy; fall back to get_variable_defs + screenshots if no seat.
Parent: Build the component system on React Aria

Title: Connect MCP servers
Description: Epic: connect and verify Figma, Linear, Notion, GitHub, Playwright via claude mcp add / OAuth.

Title: Connect Playwright MCP
Description: claude mcp add playwright npx @playwright/mcp@latest. Verify the design/qa agents can drive a browser.

Title: Connect Figma MCP and authenticate
Description: Add the remote Figma MCP (https://mcp.figma.com/mcp), authenticate via OAuth in /mcp. Confirm get_design_context and get_variable_defs work.

Title: Connect Linear MCP and authenticate
Description: Add the remote Linear MCP (https://mcp.linear.app/mcp), authenticate via OAuth. Confirm issue create/update works.

Title: Connect Notion MCP and authenticate
Description: Add the remote Notion MCP (https://mcp.notion.com/mcp), authenticate via OAuth, share the target space with the integration.

Title: Connect GitHub MCP
Description: Add the GitHub MCP (or rely on gh CLI). Confirm PR/issue/actions access, read-only where possible.

Title: Run /seed-linear to create the backlog
Description: Once Linear is connected, run /seed-linear to create this project and all tasks. (Meta: this very backlog.)

Title: Run /seed-notion to publish docs
Description: Once Notion is connected, run /seed-notion to publish the research reports and build journal.

Title: Set up the CI/CD loop
Description: Epic: GitHub Actions for lint + Vitest + Playwright a11y + Lighthouse CI, plus deploy to Cloudflare.

Title: Author CI workflow
Description: ci.yml: install, lint (jsx-a11y), Vitest, build, @axe-core/playwright suite (own project, upload report), Lighthouse CI with budgets. Fail on new critical/serious axe violations.

Title: Connect Cloudflare Pages Git integration
Description: Connect the repo in the Cloudflare dashboard as a Pages project (NOT Workers): production deploy on main, automatic preview URL per PR/branch. Static path (output: static, no adapter). Do not attach michaelstates.com yet — deploy to *.pages.dev as staging. Add public/_headers for caching+security.

Title: Cut over to michaelstates.com
Description: When the staged site looks right: attach the custom domain to the Pages project, set canonical redirect, provision HTTPS, publish the accessibility statement. This replaces the old live site.

Title: Wire Linear <-> GitHub linkage
Description: Enable branch/PR auto-linking and magic words (Fixes ENG-123). Keep state automations conservative — Done only on merge to main.

Title: Run one feature through the full loop
Description: Pick one small feature: /scope-ticket → branch → Plan Mode build → PR with Fixes → CI green → design+a11y review → merge → deploy → build-journal entry. Prove the system end to end before scaling.

Title: Establish accessibility verification baseline
Description: Epic: the three-layer a11y posture — agent review, Storybook addon-a11y, CI axe/Lighthouse — plus manual screen-reader passes.

Title: Document the accessibility pipeline as a case study
Description: Write up the a11y CI + agent-review approach as a portfolio case study. This is the signature competency — make it visible.

Title: Manual screen-reader pass (VoiceOver + NVDA)
Description: Run the primary flows through VoiceOver and NVDA. Record findings in docs/a11y/. Automation catches a minority of issues — this is where expertise shows.

Title: Publish an accessibility statement
Description: Add a public accessibility statement to the site documenting conformance target (WCAG 2.2 AA) and known limitations.

Title: Launch readiness (meta
Description: sharing
Estimate: Epic: the small set of launch-critical, audience-shaped tasks — make the site share well when the URL is forwarded, be findable by name, and confirm visits — without over-investing in SEO this low-volume/high-intent audience doesn't need.

Title: Add Open Graph / link-preview cards
Description: High leverage for this traffic model: the URL gets pasted into emails and forwarded between people, so the unfurled card is the first impression before any click. Set per-page title, description, and a designed OG image; verify rendering when the link is shared. Astro has good patterns for this (e.g. generated OG images).

Title: Set up lightweight privacy-respecting analytics
Description: Just enough to confirm people visited — not a conversion funnel (volume is too low for that). Self-hosted Umami on your own server is free and on-ethos for an accessibility person; Plausible/Fathom if you prefer hosted. No cookie banner needed with these.

Title: Ensure basic indexability
Description: Minimal SEO only: a sitemap, sensible meta description, and robots config so a search of your name surfaces the site. Skip keyword optimization — organic discovery is rare for this audience.
